expectations.text: SHA-pinned all GitHub Actions references (uses: owner/action@sha # vX.Y.Z format)
expectations.evidence: All five action references in the workflow YAML use full 40-char SHA pins with version comments: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2, actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405 # v6.2.0, pre-commit/action@2c7b3805fd2a0fd8c1884dcaebf91fc102a13ecd # v3.0.1, DavidAnson/markdownlint-cli2-action@07035fd053f7be764496c0f8d8f9f41f98305101 # v22.0.0, streetsidesoftware/cspell-action@9cd41bb518a24fefdafd9880cbab8f0ceba04d28 # v8.3.0.

expectations.text: Used live sources (GitHub API or WebSearch) to verify action versions exist
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows multiple WebFetch calls to live GitHub releases pages for each action. WebSearch was also used for version lookups.

expectations.text: Checked for CVEs/security advisories on referenced actions or tools
expectations.evidence: No CVE-specific searches or GitHub advisory database queries appear in the transcript. The report discusses supply chain risks conceptually (referencing tj-actions compromise) but does not show any active check for known CVEs on the specific actions or tools referenced.

expectations.text: Used the decision format for version choices where the project has options
expectations.evidence: Section 4 covers architectural choices with pros/cons rationale but no structured decision format was applied to version selection choices. Version-selection tradeoffs are not surfaced as decisions with alternatives.

expectations.text: Included a verification log table at the end
expectations.evidence: The report ends with a Sources section listing reference URLs, not a verification log table. Section 3 contains a Verified Action SHAs table mid-document, but it only lists action name, tag, and SHA — not a verification log with source URL, verification method, or result.
